body: tracker.update_issue() gates the claim-eligibility check on 'target_state == "claimed" and issue.state != "claimed"' (tracker.py ~line 821). When a ticket is already in state=claimed and someone runs 'gittoc claim T-X' (or 'update --state claimed --owner other'), the check is skipped, the owner field is overwritten, and a fresh 'claimed' event is appended — with no warning. This effectively lets one owner silently steal another owner's claim. Options: (a) block re-claim by a different owner unless --force is passed, (b) emit an audit-style event that records the previous owner, or (c) at minimum warn to stderr when the owner changes.
title: re-claim of already-claimed ticket silently steals ownership
